title: Python for DevOps
description: A focused, practical course on using Python for DevOps and infrastructure automation. Starting with a quick refresher on Python fundamentals, you will learn file and system operations, process automation, API integration, configuration management, Docker automation, AWS scripting with boto3, and building robust CLI tools. Every module emphasizes real-world automation scenarios.

modules.title: Python Basics Refresher
modules.description: A fast-paced review of Python fundamentals: data types, control flow, functions, list comprehensions, and virtual environments.
objectives: Set up a Python virtual environment with venv
Use core data types: strings, lists, dicts, sets
Write functions with type hints
Apply list and dict comprehensions for concise data transformation

modules.title: File and System Operations
modules.description: Read, write, and manipulate files using pathlib and os. Work with file permissions, directory traversal, and temporary files.
objectives: Navigate the filesystem with pathlib.Path
Read and write files in text and binary modes
Walk directory trees and match files with glob patterns
Handle file permissions and temporary files

modules.title: Process Automation with subprocess
modules.description: Execute shell commands from Python, capture output, handle errors, and build automation pipelines using the subprocess module.
objectives: Run external commands with subprocess.run
Capture stdout and stderr from processes
Handle process exit codes and timeouts
Chain commands and build shell-like pipelines in Python

modules.title: API Integration with Requests
modules.description: Make HTTP requests, handle authentication, parse responses, and interact with REST APIs using the requests library.
objectives: Make GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE requests
Handle authentication: API keys, tokens, and basic auth
Parse JSON responses and handle errors gracefully
Implement retry logic and timeouts

modules.title: YAML and JSON Config Parsing
modules.description: Parse, generate, and validate configuration files in YAML and JSON formats. Work with Jinja2 templates for config generation.
objectives: Parse and write YAML files with PyYAML
Load and dump JSON with the json module
Validate configuration structure programmatically
Generate configuration files from Jinja2 templates

modules.title: Docker Automation with Python
modules.description: Control Docker programmatically using the Docker SDK for Python. Build images, manage containers, and automate Docker workflows.
objectives: Connect to the Docker daemon with the Docker SDK
Build images and manage containers programmatically
Inspect container logs, stats, and networks
Automate multi-container workflows

modules.title: AWS Automation with boto3
modules.description: Interact with AWS services using boto3. Manage EC2 instances, S3 buckets, and IAM resources through Python scripts.
objectives: Configure boto3 with credentials and regions
Manage EC2 instances: launch, stop, terminate, and describe
Work with S3: upload, download, list, and manage buckets
Handle AWS API errors and implement pagination

modules.title: Infrastructure Scripting Patterns
modules.description: Combine file operations, subprocess, APIs, and cloud SDKs to build real infrastructure automation scripts: health checks, log aggregation, and deployment helpers.
objectives: Build a server health check script with retry logic
Automate log collection and aggregation across hosts
Script a deployment pipeline with rollback capability
Implement idempotent infrastructure operations

modules.title: Testing with pytest
modules.description: Write reliable tests for your automation scripts using pytest. Use fixtures, mocking, parametrize, and test infrastructure code safely.
objectives: Write test functions and use assert statements with pytest
Use fixtures for setup and teardown
Mock external services with unittest.mock and pytest-mock
Parametrize tests for multiple input scenarios

modules.title: Building CLI Tools with Click
modules.description: Build professional command-line tools using Click. Add commands, options, arguments, help text, and output formatting.
objectives: Create CLI commands with Click decorators
Add options, arguments, and help text
Implement subcommands for complex tools
Format output with rich or tabulate for readability

modules.title: Capstone: Build a DevOps Automation Toolkit
modules.description: Build a complete CLI toolkit that automates a real DevOps workflow: check infrastructure health, deploy a containerized app, and report status via API.
objectives: Design a multi-command CLI tool for DevOps automation
Integrate Docker, API calls, and config parsing in one tool
Implement error handling, logging, and dry-run modes
Write tests for the core automation logic
